description.content: Endpoints for evaluating feature flags and retrieving treatment values for given keys and feature flag names.

item.name: Get treatment for a feature flag

request.name: Get treatment for a feature flag

description.content: Evaluates a single feature flag for the given key and returns the treatment value. Optionally includes dynamic configuration associated with the treatment. This is the primary endpoint for feature flag evaluation.

query.description: (Required) The customer key to evaluate the feature flag against
(Required) The name of the feature flag to evaluate
Optional bucketing key used for consistent percentage-based treatment assignments
JSON string of attributes used in targeting rule evaluation
When set to true, disables impression logging for this evaluation

description.content: Evaluates a single feature flag for the given key with attributes passed in the request body. This is preferred over the GET method when attributes contain complex or large data structures.

query.description: (Required) The customer key to evaluate the feature flag against
(Required) The name of the feature flag to evaluate
Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments

item.name: Get treatments for multiple feature flags

request.name: Get treatments for multiple feature flags

description.content: Evaluates multiple feature flags for the given key in a single request and returns the treatment values for each. More efficient than making individual get-treatment calls.

query.description: (Required) The customer key to evaluate feature flags against
(Required) Comma-separated list of feature flag names to evaluate
Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments
JSON string of attributes for targeting rule evaluation
When set to true, disables impression logging for these evaluations

response.name: Successful evaluation response with multiple treatments

description.content: Evaluates multiple feature flags for the given key with attributes passed in the request body.

query.description: (Required) The customer key to evaluate feature flags against
(Required) Comma-separated list of feature flag names to evaluate
Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments

response.name: Successful evaluation response with multiple treatments

item.name: Get all treatments for a key

request.name: Get all treatments for a key

description.content: Evaluates all available feature flags for the given keys and traffic types and returns the treatment values. Useful for retrieving a complete set of flag evaluations for a user session.

query.description: (Required) JSON string containing an array of key objects with matchingKey and trafficType properties, and optionally a bucketingKey
JSON string of attributes for targeting rule evaluation
When set to true, disables impression logging for these evaluations

response.name: Successful response with treatments for all feature flags

description.content: Evaluates all available feature flags for the given keys with attributes passed in the request body.

query.description: (Required) JSON string containing an array of key objects

response.name: Successful response with treatments for all feature flags

description.content: Endpoints for tracking custom events used in experimentation and metrics measurement.
Records a custom event for the given key and event type. Events are used to measure the impact of feature flags on user actions and experimentation metrics.

query.description: (Required) The customer key associated with the event
(Required) The traffic type for the event
(Required) The type of event being tracked (e.g., purchase, click, signup)
Optional numeric value associated with the event
JSON string with up to 15 key-value pairs of event properties. Values must be boolean, string, number, or null.

description.content: Records a custom event with properties passed in the request body. Preferred for events with complex property structures.

query.description: (Required) The customer key associated with the event
(Required) The traffic type for the event
(Required) The type of event being tracked
Optional numeric value associated with the event

description.content: Checks whether the Split Evaluator service is running. If the service is running, it responds with the text pong and HTTP status code 200.
Returns the current version of the Split Evaluator service.

Checks that the Split Evaluator is connected to Split servers and ready to process evaluation requests. Returns 200 if everything is working as expected or 500 if there is an issue, along with a message explaining the status.

response.name: Service is healthy and ready for evaluations

description.content:
The Split Evaluator is an HTTP service that wraps Split SDKs to provide feature flag evaluation via a REST API. It enables applications that cannot directly embed an SDK to request treatment evaluations over HTTP, making it suitable for architectures where a centralized evaluation service is preferred. The Evaluator can be deployed as a standalone microservice that processes getTreatment calls and returns the appropriate treatments for given users and feature flags. Swagger documentation is available by default at the /api-docs endpoint.

Contact Support:
 Name: Split Support
